dataProvenance.toleratedTemp: Murchie et al. 2011 — lower lethal 13.6°C; Brownscombe et al. CTMax 37.9°C: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0306456510001233
dataProvenance.depthPreference: FishBase 0-84m + flats feeding <1ft (Florida Museum) + Rezek 2025 juvenile 1.8-2.5m: https://academic.oup.com/mcf/article/17/3/vtaf003/8137700
dataProvenance.tidePreference: Ultrasonic telemetry (Bahamas) — rising-tide flats feeding documented: https://www.bonefishtarpontrust.org/blog/2017-07-27-bonefish-and-tides/
dataProvenance.lunarSensitivity: BTT acoustic-tagging — pre-spawn aggregations on full + new moons fall-spring: https://www.bonefishtarpontrust.org/bonefish-life-cycle/
dataProvenance.weatherSensitivity: Cold-front hazard documented (2010-2011 die-offs); frontalPassageFactor calibrated from population-mortality lit: https://academic.oup.com/mcf/article/17/3/vtaf003/8137700
dataProvenance.timeOfDayPreferences: Visual sight feeders requiring daylight (Florida Museum); midday avoidance qualitatively documented: https://www.floridamuseum.ufl.edu/discover-fish/species-profiles/bonefish/
dataProvenance.structurePreferences: FWC + Florida Museum: intertidal sand/grass flats → sandFlat, mangrove zones → weedLine: https://myfwc.com/fishing/saltwater/recreational/bonefish/
dataProvenance.weeklyPresenceCurve: FWC peak Mar-Oct + Boucek/Santos 2018 post-2010 cold-event population data: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10641-018-0820-5
dataProvenance.baitAssociations: Crabtree et al. NOAA SPO Florida Keys feeding-habits study: https://spo.nmfs.noaa.gov/content/feeding-habits-bonefish-albula-vulpes-waters-florida-keys
